message:
fix: case-insensitive WebSocket URL schemes, worker runtime contexts, tokio-compat cancel semantics

WebSocket URL normalization (asupersync-browser-core/src/lib.rs):

  normalize_websocket_url() now accepts mixed-case schemes (WSS://,
  Ws://, etc.) by splitting on :// and comparing the scheme with
  eq_ignore_ascii_case. The output lowercases the scheme to ensure
  consistent downstream matching. Previously only exact "ws://" and
  "wss://" prefixes were accepted.

  Test added (abi_exports.rs): websocket_url_validation_accepts_mixed_
  case_ws_schemes verifies WSS:// and Ws:// both open successfully and
  pass diagnostics.

Browser runtime contexts (packages/browser/src/index.ts):

  Add "service_worker" and "shared_worker" to BrowserRuntimeContext
  union type. browserRuntimeContext() now checks for these host roles
  before falling through to the generic detection path.

  detectBrowserSharedWorkerCoordinatorSupport() now uses a dedicated
  directRuntimeReason constant ("shared_worker_not_yet_shipped") instead
  of reusing runtimeSupport.reason, ensuring the fail-closed reason code
  is correctly attributed to the shared-worker host path.

Tokio-compat cancel semantics (asupersync-tokio-compat/src/runtime.rs):

  with_tokio_context() now returns None whenever cancellation was
  observed, even if the wrapped future reports Ready on the same poll
  where cancellation becomes visible. Previously CancellationIgnored
  returned Some(value), which violated the contract that cancellation
  observation implies None. CancellationIgnored is now treated the same
  as Cancelled.

  Test added: test_with_tokio_context_returns_none_when_cancel_observed_
  before_ready verifies that a future that cancels itself and then
  becomes ready on the next poll still returns None.

Validation script + contract tests:

  - validate_rust_browser_consumer.sh: mirror browser_run fields into
    summary.json including service_worker_fail_closed_reason_code,
    shared_worker_fail_closed_reason_code, and downgrade_reason_code
  - README.md: document the summary.json mirroring
  - wasm_rust_browser_example_contract.rs: assert new markers
  - wasm_service_worker_broker_contract.rs: assert hostRole guard
  - wasm_shared_worker_tenancy_lifecycle_contract.rs: assert hostRole
    guard, directRuntimeReason constant, shared_worker_not_yet_shipped

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 set -euo pipefail
 
-# beads: asupersync-4l9iw.2, asupersync-4l9iw.8
+# beads: asupersync-4l9iw.2, asupersync-4l9iw.8, asupersync-4l9iw.11
 
 SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
 REPO_ROOT="$(cd -- "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
@@ -93,6 +93,20 @@ summary = {

     "status": "pass",
+    "browser_run": {

+        "service_worker_fail_closed_reason_code": browser_run["service_worker_fail_closed_reason_code"],
+        "shared_worker_fail_closed_reason_code": browser_run["shared_worker_fail_closed_reason_code"],
+        "downgrade_selected_lane": browser_run["downgrade_selected_lane"],
+        "downgrade_browser_selection_lane": browser_run["downgrade_browser_selection_lane"],
+        "downgrade_reason_code": browser_run["downgrade_reason_code"],
+        "dedicated_worker_selected_lane": browser_run["dedicated_worker_selected_lane"],
+        "dedicated_worker_browser_selection_lane": browser_run["dedicated_worker_browser_selection_lane"],
+    },
     "checks": {
         "dist_exists": dist.exists(),
         "index_html_exists": (dist / "index.html").exists(),

@@ -15,6 +15,7 @@ The validation script:
 - builds the nested Rust crate with `rch exec -- wasm-pack build ...`
 - stages the generated `pkg/` output next to the copied frontend consumer
 - runs a Vite bundle check against the resulting browser artifacts
+- mirrors `browser-run.json` into `summary.json`, including `service_worker_fail_closed_reason_code`, `shared_worker_fail_closed_reason_code`, and `downgrade_reason_code`, so the synthetic unsupported-worker evidence stays visible in the top-level QA artifact
 - runs a real browser matrix that proves:
   - browser main-thread lifecycle + execution-ladder diagnostics
   - dedicated-worker lifecycle + execution-ladder diagnostics
